title: all credHelpers run all the time
body:
### Issue Description

With docker and credHelpers scripts, the script is only called when necessary.
With podman all credHelpers scripts for every registry are run, regardless of whether the current command matches that registry.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:

Create a few credHelpers scripts in ~/config/containers/auth.json

```
  "credHelpers": {
    "x.com": "debugX",
    "y.com": "debugY",
    "z.com": "debugZ",
  }
```

The scripts look like `docker-credential-debugX` in the PATH and contain something like:
```
echo "Logging into registry x.com ($1)" > /dev/stderr
echo "{}"
```

Run `podman pull docker.io/library/busybox`

podman output:

> Logging into registry z.com (get)
Logging into registry z.com (get)
Logging into registry x.com (get)
Logging into registry x.com (get)
Logging into registry y.com (get)
Logging into registry y.com (get)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/busybox:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:c2bf9493c1bf786e95e3eac7d406c20aa1b8a2d40916756e891627e9e8f8d119
Copying config sha256:3e4fd538a9a0b729be05707cf805388be2fb701cfd5d44c6542f1988e8aef6e3
Writing manifest to image destination
3e4fd538a9a0b729be05707cf805388be2fb701cfd5d44c6542f1988e8aef6e3



### Describe the results you received

Every credHelper script is called regardless of whether the registry matches

### Describe the results you expected

I expect none of my credHelper scripts to be called when the image doesn't match the registry

comments.body: @mtrmac this is a containers/image issue correct?

This is mostly an ~intentional Podman behavior / missing Podman feature: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11636#issuecomment-965902003

The remote client must collect all credentials, because credentials for mirrors might be necessary, and the remote client doesn’t know what are the possible mirrors.

> This is mostly an ~intentional Podman behavior / missing Podman feature: [#11636 (comment)](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11636#issuecomment-965902003)
> 
> The remote client must collect all credentials, because credentials for mirrors might be necessary, and the remote client doesn’t know what are the possible mirrors.

I don't fully understand this.

Presumably docker works in a similar way and yet doesn't exhibit this behaviour.
I'm assuming the docker client can determine the appropriate registry from whatever image is being passed on the command line options.

I should also add that podman executes every single credHelper script when calling `podman run` on a local image, not just `podman pull`.
The image being run could be built locally or prefetched, it doesn't matter, podman will always execute all the credHelpers.

I can't see how that is desirable.

Oh it’s not _desirable_, but the feature to determine which credentials to provide does not exist, so this is the best we could have done at that time.

(And even if we could determine which 3 mirrors could possibly be relevant, the current design would still ask for credentials of all 3 before beginning the operation. _Interactively_ asking for credentials only when they are really needed would require a fairly large restructuring, across 3 repos and a client/server protocol.)

Docker, reportedly, does not support authentication when using mirrors _at all_.

> Interactively

I'm probably misunderstanding the use of interactively here.
I'm probably also lacking knowledge of 'mirrors'

I don't expect any interactivity in credHelpers (I doubt docker supports interactivity).
I do expect that x.com credHelper script is only run if the image name starts with x.com

I'm assuming mirrors means that x.com/foo could be fetched from somewhere other than x.com
I'm unfamiliar with this feature.

I'm also affected by this. In my situation there are `credHelpers` configured that are only accessible on some networks, so when I'm not connected to one of those networks, the credhelper will attempt to connect and eventually hit a timeout, which leads to a very poor UX with podman.

I'd be happy with a workaround that would disable `credHelpers` altogether via an env var or podman global setting, do you have any advice where I could add that in the code? I believe maybe somewhere around [here](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/46dd8f5dd18598989216c23a8d9d357486565190/pkg/docker/config/config.go#L67)

I'd also be interested in knowing more about how "mirrors" prevents this, maybe a better workaround would be to emulate what docker does and not use `credHelpers` for mirror requests? (behind a config option potentially) 

Either way, the current design makes podman pretty frustrating in my environment (every `pull` takes a long time, just for each `credHelper` invocation to timeout).
To avoid this issue I use a small workaround: `podman --config /path/to/emptyconfig <command>` where `/path/to/emptyconfig` contains an empty `config.json` (it contains `{}`)
